title: US Measles Outbreak Hits New Record as Health Experts Raise Concerns
subtitle: The country has reported its highest number of infections since 1991, with officials warning that the true scale of the crisis remains unknown.

summary.en: The United States has officially recorded more measles cases in 2026 than in all of 2025. New data from the CDC shows 2,318 cases, making this the worst year for the disease since 1991. Health experts warn that vaccination rates have fallen below the level needed to stop the virus from spreading. Many researchers believe the actual number of infections is much higher than official reports suggest. There are growing concerns about the nation's ability to manage this and other infectious disease threats.
summary.zh: 美國二〇二六年正式記錄的麻疹病例數已超過二〇二五年全年。來自美國疾病管制與預防中心的最新數據顯示共有二千三百一十八起病例，使今年成為自一九九一年以來該疾病最嚴重的一年。衛生專家警告，疫苗接種率已降至防止病毒傳播所需的門檻以下。許多研究人員認為，實際感染人數遠高於官方報告。外界對於美國管理此次疫情及其他傳染病威脅的能力感到日益擔憂。

paragraphs.en: The United States has officially surpassed last year’s record number of measles infections, confirming that 2026 is now the worst year for the disease since 1991. According to new figures released by the US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC), the country has recorded 2,318 cases so far. This number is already higher than the 2,289 cases reported during the entire year of 2025, even though there are still five months left in 2026.
paragraphs.zh: 美國已正式超越去年的麻疹感染紀錄，證實二〇二六年成為自一九九一年以來該疾病最嚴重的一年。根據美國疾病管制與預防中心（CDC）發布的最新數據，該國目前已記錄到二千三百一十八起病例。儘管二〇二六年還剩下五個月，但此數字已高於二〇二五年全年度通報的二千二百八十九起病例。

paragraphs.en: This record-breaking rise follows a period where the country crossed the 2,000-case threshold last month. Large outbreaks in states including South Carolina, Utah, and Arizona have caused hundreds of infections, while smaller clusters have appeared in other parts of the country. International health officials are expected to meet in November to decide if the US and Mexico should lose their official status as "measles-free" nations. The US achieved this status in 2000, but it relies on maintaining high vaccination rates to prevent the virus from returning.
paragraphs.zh: 這波破紀錄的增長發生在該國上個月突破二千例門檻之後。包括南卡羅來納州、猶他州和亞利桑那州在內的多個州爆發大規模疫情，導致數百人感染，同時國內其他地區也出現了較小規模的群聚感染。國際衛生官員預計將於十一月開會，決定美國與墨西哥是否應失去其作為「無麻疹」國家的官方地位。美國於二〇〇〇年取得此地位，但這仰賴維持高疫苗接種率以防止病毒捲土重來。

paragraphs.en: Experts note that the country has fallen below the 95% vaccination coverage level considered necessary to stop outbreaks. According to MedPage Today, national vaccination rates for measles, mumps, and rubella dropped to 92.5% among kindergartners in the 2024-2025 school year, down from 95.2% in 2019-2020. These national averages can be misleading, as some local communities have even lower rates, which makes it easier for the virus to spread.
paragraphs.zh: 專家指出，該國的疫苗接種覆蓋率已低於阻斷疫情爆發所需的百分之九十五門檻。根據《醫學網頁今日》（MedPage Today）報導，二〇二四至二〇二五學年度幼兒園學童的麻疹、腮腺炎及德國麻疹疫苗接種率降至百分之九十二點五，低於二〇一九至二〇二〇學年度的百分之九十五點二。這些全國平均數據可能具有誤導性，因為部分地方社區的接種率甚至更低，這使得病毒更容易傳播。

paragraphs.en: While the official count is high, public health officials and disease researchers warn that the true scope of the outbreak is likely much larger. Many people who get sick never visit a doctor, meaning their cases are never added to the official total. In South Carolina, which experienced the nation's largest outbreak in 35 years, health officials have admitted that their count of 997 cases is likely an undercount. Similarly, researchers studying a past outbreak in Texas found that the actual number of cases may have been nearly twice as high as the 762 reported at the time.
paragraphs.zh: 雖然官方統計數字很高，但公共衛生官員與疾病研究人員警告，疫情的真實規模可能大得多。許多生病的人從未就醫，這意味著他們的病例從未被計入官方總數。在經歷了該國三十五年來最大規模疫情的南卡羅來納州，衛生官員承認其統計的九百九十七起病例很可能被低估了。同樣地，研究德州過去疫情的研究人員發現，實際病例數可能比當時通報的七百六十二起高出近兩倍。

paragraphs.en: In Utah, state epidemiologist Leisha Nolen noted that mathematical models suggest a large outbreak on the border with Arizona could be three to four times bigger than what is currently known. Because measles is one of the most contagious viruses known to medicine, these gaps in data make it difficult for officials to track the disease accurately. Damon Toth, an applied mathematician at the University of Utah, explained that without a consistent way to measure the severity of cases across all states, it is hard to estimate the true size of the outbreak.
paragraphs.zh: 在猶他州，州流行病學家諾倫（Leisha Nolen）指出，數學模型顯示亞利桑那州邊境的大規模疫情，其規模可能是目前已知的三到四倍。由於麻疹是醫學界已知傳染力最強的病毒之一，這些數據缺口使得官員難以精確追蹤該疾病。猶他大學的應用數學家托斯（Damon Toth）解釋說，若缺乏一套在各州之間衡量病例嚴重程度的一致方法，就很難估計疫情的真實規模。

paragraphs.en: The American Academy of Pediatrics has expressed strong concern, calling the situation an "avoidable crisis" that hurts children the most. The organization noted that these cases represent real people, including babies hospitalized with pneumonia and students who have missed weeks of school. They warned that the country should not accept this situation as a new normal.
paragraphs.zh: 美國兒科學會（American Academy of Pediatrics）表達了強烈擔憂，稱此情況為一場「可避免的危機」，且對兒童傷害最深。該組織指出，這些病例代表的是真實的人，包括因肺炎住院的嬰兒以及缺課數週的學生。他們警告，該國不應接受這種情況成為新常態。

paragraphs.en: However, changing this trend may be difficult. The Guardian Health reports that the US health secretary, Robert F. Kennedy Jr., has a history of downplaying the importance of vaccines and overstating their risks. Former CDC officials have also expressed concern that the public health system has been weakened by recent cuts to staffing and funding. This comes at a time when the US is facing multiple health threats, including bird flu, whooping cough, and Ebola, raising questions about the nation's ability to contain infectious diseases.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，要改變這種趨勢可能很困難。《衛報健康》（The Guardian Health）報導稱，美國衛生部長小羅伯特·甘迺迪（Robert F. Kennedy Jr.）過去曾有淡化疫苗重要性並誇大其風險的紀錄。前CDC官員也表示擔憂，認為公共衛生體系因近期的人事與資金削減而遭到削弱。此時正值美國面臨包括禽流感、百日咳與伊波拉等多重健康威脅，引發了外界對該國控制傳染病能力的質疑。

paragraphs.en: As the country moves toward the end of the year, the focus remains on whether vaccination rates can be improved. Without a nationwide effort to increase immunity, health experts fear that measles could once again become a regularly circulating disease in the United States, potentially sickening thousands of people every year. For now, the focus of health departments remains on tracking the virus and managing the impact on local communities.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著該國邁向年底，焦點仍集中在疫苗接種率是否能獲得改善。若沒有全國性的努力來提升免疫力，衛生專家擔心麻疹可能再次成為美國定期傳播的疾病，每年可能導致數千人染病。目前，衛生部門的重點仍放在追蹤病毒以及管理其對地方社區的影響。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To pass a specific point, level, or limit.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 超過、跨越（某個門檻或數值）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「橫越」馬路，這裡指數據上的突破。文中：This record-breaking rise follows a period where the country crossed the 2,000-case threshold last month.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To make something seem less important or serious than it really is.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 輕描淡寫、貶低……的重要性。
keyTerms.noteZh: 由 down 和 play 組成，字面意思容易誤導，實際指刻意淡化某事的嚴重性。文中：The Guardian Health reports that the US health secretary, Robert F. Kennedy Jr., has a history of downplaying the importance of vaccines and overstating their risks.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Missing parts or information in a set of data or a system.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「縫隙」或「差距」，這裡指數據收集上的不完整。文中：Because measles is one of the most contagious viruses known to medicine, these gaps in data make it difficult for officials to track the disease accurately.

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: US surpasses 2025’s record number of measles infections as cases climb

sources.name: MedPage Today
sources.title: U.S. Measles Case Count Breaks Record

previousArticles.title: US Measles Cases Reach Highest Level in Decades
previousArticles.summaryEn: The United States has officially recorded more measles cases in 2026 than in any year since 1991. The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) reported 2,318 cases, surpassing the previous record set in 2025. Health experts warn that the country may lose its status as a measles-free nation due to low vaccination rates. The American Academy of Pediatrics has called the situation an avoidable crisis that harms children. Officials worry that the current public health system is not prepared to stop the spread of this disease.

previousArticles.title: Ebola Crisis in Congo Surpasses 1,000 Deaths
previousArticles.summaryEn: The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo has now claimed over 1,000 lives. Health experts report that this is the fastest-spreading Ebola epidemic ever recorded. Medical teams are facing major challenges, including armed conflict and a lack of funding. Many people are avoiding treatment centers due to fear and social stigma. Researchers are now exploring whether existing vaccines might offer protection against the specific virus strain currently spreading.

previousArticles.title: U.S. Faces Record-Breaking Parasite Outbreak
previousArticles.summaryEn: The United States is currently experiencing a record-breaking outbreak of a parasite called Cyclospora. Health agencies have reported over 11,500 total cases across 41 states. Officials are investigating links between the illness and contaminated lettuce from Mexico. Experts warn that budget cuts and staff shortages are slowing down the response. Despite the high number of cases, officials remain divided on the severity of the situation.

quiz.question: What is the specific change in measles vaccination rates among kindergartners mentioned in the article?

options.A: They increased from 92.5% in 2019 to 95.2% in 2025.
options.B: They dropped from 95.2% in 2019-2020 to 92.5% in 2024-2025.
options.C: They have remained steady at 95% since the year 2000.
options.D: They fell below the 90% threshold for the first time in 2026.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，文中明確指出全國幼兒園學生的麻疹、腮腺炎和德國麻疹疫苗接種率從 2019-2020 學年的 95.2% 下降至 2024-2025 學年的 92.5%。選項 A 將數據順序顛倒；選項 C 與文中提到的「跌破 95%」事實不符；選項 D 雖然提到 95%，但並非文中描述的具體下降數值。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the official CDC case counts compared to the actual number of measles infections?

options.A: The official counts are likely inflated due to over-reporting by local hospitals.
options.B: The official counts are perfectly accurate because they include all suspected cases.
options.C: The actual number of infections is likely significantly higher than the reported figures.
options.D: The official counts only include cases from states with the largest outbreaks.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據文章第四段與第五段，公共衛生官員指出許多生病的人並未就醫，且南卡羅來納州與德州的案例研究顯示，實際感染人數可能遠高於官方統計數據（如德州案例可能為兩倍，猶他州模型甚至預估為三到四倍）。選項 A 與文意相反；選項 B 被文中提到的數據落差所反駁；選項 D 錯誤，因為官方統計包含全國範圍，而非僅限於大爆發地區。

quiz.question: In the context of the seventh paragraph, what does the word 'downplaying' mean?

options.A: To make something seem less important or serious than it really is.
options.B: To physically lower the position of an object or a person.
options.C: To provide evidence that contradicts a popular scientific belief.
options.D: To increase the amount of funding for a specific health program.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。文中提到衛生部長 Robert F. Kennedy Jr. 對疫苗持懷疑態度，並有「downplaying」疫苗重要性的紀錄，這在語境中意指他刻意淡化或輕視疫苗的重要性。選項 B 是字面上的誤解；選項 C 雖然在討論疫苗爭議，但 downplaying 本身不等於提供反證；選項 D 與語意完全相反。

quiz.question: What is the primary message regarding the current measles situation in the United States?

options.A: The US has successfully contained the measles virus through new vaccination mandates.
options.B: Measles is no longer a threat to children because of advancements in medical treatment.
options.C: The US is facing a significant, avoidable public health crisis due to declining vaccination rates and systemic challenges.
options.D: The rise in measles cases is solely due to international travelers arriving from Mexico.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章整體論述了美國麻疹病例創下 1991 年以來新高，並分析了原因，包括接種率下降、公共衛生系統資源不足以及官員對疫苗的態度，並強調這是一場「可避免的危機」。選項 A 與事實不符；選項 B 忽略了文章對麻疹傳染性與危險性的警告；選項 D 將問題過度簡化，文中並未將責任歸咎於單一因素。
